data.title: The Sand-Net Reset
data.description: High-intensity physical exertion via morning beach volleyball acts as a neurological 'hard reset,' consistently clearing cognitive fatigue and enabling peak-performance coding sessions within 24-48 hours.

data.title: The Perfectionism Stalling Trap
data.description: When faced with complex architectural bugs or backend integration hurdles, the user subconsciously pivots to 'secondary perfectionism'—obsessing over slide deck aesthetics or jargon—to avoid the discomfort of solving the actual technical problem.

data.title: The 3:30 AM Cognitive Debt
data.description: Late-night hacking sessions extending past 3:30 AM create a 'conceptual hangover' that manifests as severe mental blocks and an inability to visualize basic algorithmic structures for the following 48 hours.

data.title: The Academic-Development Cross-Contamination
data.description: Rigorous study of low-level CS algorithms (paging, B-Trees) creates a mental fatigue that bleeds into full-stack development, causing the user to perceive standard debugging tasks as insurmountable 'cognitive walls.'

data.title: The Cinematic Palate Cleanser
data.description: The user utilizes immersive, large-scale cinematic experiences as a deliberate, high-effort coping mechanism to break out of intense technical debugging loops, effectively resetting their focus at the cost of a full day's productivity.
